name: dumbbell incline one arm fly
instructions: Adjust the incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on your thigh.
Lie back on the bench, keeping your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the dumbbell with your arm extended straight up over your chest.
Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the motion to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

Adjust the incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle. Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on your thigh. Lie back on the bench, keeping your feet flat on the ground. Hold the dumbbell with your arm extended straight up over your chest. Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow. Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the motion to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
